Market Analysis: • Price at $614.23 is below all major moving averages with a deep Death Cross and MACD at -6.51, indicating a dominant bearish structure.
• Early stabilization signals include RSI bullish divergence from 39.17, slowing MACD decline, and declining ATR from 21.54 to 17.06.
• A HOLD is recommended as the stock attempts to base between $598 and $618 with key resistance at the 50 SMA ($622) and support at $598.
News Analysis: • U.S. April CPI surged to 3.8% YoY, crushing hopes for near-term Fed rate cuts and shifting market odds toward a potential rate hike.
• META benefits from a strong AI capex supercycle and massive cash reserves, which act as a relative safe haven in a higher-rate environment.
• Inflation eroding consumer real wages and rising energy costs pose headwinds for META’s ad revenue and data center expenses.
Fundamentals: • Meta shows accelerating revenue growth (33.1% YoY in Q1 2026) with record quarterly EPS of $10.44 and best-in-class operating margins above 40%.
• The company is in an unprecedented infrastructure buildout, spending 34-35% of revenue on capex, which has shifted its balance sheet from net cash to net debt.

• Valuation appears attractive with a forward P/E of 16.97x and PEG ratio of 0.89, but risks include a paused buyback program, rising stock-based compensation, and uncertain returns from massive AI and metaverse investments.
Final Decision: • Retain Underweight rating with a 35% position trim at market to balance downside risk against stabilizing momentum signals.
• Place a hard stop-loss at $580 on the remaining position to allow room for volatility while capping catastrophic loss.
• Sell covered calls at the $635 strike for May 28 expiry to generate premium and enhance the effective exit price.
